perturbation.original_text: The Confidant must take all reasonable precautions to avoid unauthorised disclosure and protect against unauthorised use o f the Confidential Information , such precautions to be at least as stringent as those used by the Confidant for the protection of the Confidant ’s own confidential information and always in accordance with the Privacy Act 1988 .
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidant should take precautions to avoid unauthorised disclosure and protect against unauthorised use of the Confidential Information, generally in accordance with the Privacy Act 1988.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'must take all reasonable precautions' to 'should take precautions' and removing the stringency requirement makes the obligation vague and less enforceable. Removing 'at least as stringent as those used by the Confidant for the protection of the Confidant’s own confidential information' reduces security, resulting in ambiguity regarding the required level of protection.
perturbation.law_explanation: APP 11 requires entities to take active measures to ensure the security of personal information they hold and to protect it from misuse, interference and loss, and from unauthorised access, modification or disclosure. The modified clause weakens this obligation to a mere 'should take precautions,' making the level of protection unclear and potentially non-compliant with the active and robust security measures required by APP 11.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: anagement of personal information Ensures that APP entities manage personal information in an open and transparent way. This includes having a clearly expressed and up to date APP privacy policy . APP 2 Anonymity and pseudonymity Requires APP entities to give individuals the option of not identifying themselves, or of using a pseudonym. Limited exceptions apply. APP 3 Collection of solicited personal information Outlines when an APP entity can collect personal information that is solicited. It applies higher standards to the collection of sensitive information . APP 4 Dealing with unsolicited personal information Outlines how APP entities must deal with unsolicited personal information. APP 5 Notification of the collection of personal information Outlines when and in what circumstances an APP entity that collects personal information must tell an individual about certain matters. APP 6 Use or disclosure of personal information Outlines the circumstances in which an APP entity may use or disclose personal information that it holds. APP 7 Direct marketing An organisation may only use or disclose personal information for direct marketing purposes if certain conditions are met. APP 8 Cross-border disclosure of personal information Outlines the steps an APP entity must take to protect personal information before it is disclosed overseas. APP 9 Adoption, use or disclosure of government related identifiers Outlines the limited circumstances when an organisation may adopt a government related identifier of an individual as its own identifier, or use or disclose a government related identifier of an individual. APP 10 Quality of personal information An APP entity must take reasonable steps to ensure the personal information it collects is accurate, up to date and complete. An entity must also take reasonable steps to ensure the personal information it uses or discloses is accurate, up to date, complete and relevant, having regard to the purpose of the use or disclosure. APP 11 Security of personal information An APP entity must take reasonable steps to protect personal information it holds from misuse, interference and loss, and from unauthorised access, modification or disclosure. An entity has obligations to destroy or de-identify personal information in certain circumstances. APP 12 Access to personal information Outlines an APP entity’s obligations when an individual requests to be given access to personal information held about them by the entity. This includes a requirement to provide access unless a specific exception applies. APP 13 Correction of personal information Outlines an APP entity’s obligations in relation to correcting the personal information it holds about individuals. perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation to protect personal information, creating a contradiction with APP 11 of the Privacy Act 1988. The original text states that the Confidant 'must take all reasonable precautions,' while the modified text uses 'should take precautions,' reducing the enforceability and stringency of the security measures. The original text explicitly mentions that the precautions should be 'at least as stringent as those used by the Confidant for the protection of the Confidant’s own confidential information,' which is removed in the changed text, resulting in ambiguity. APP 11 states that an entity 'must take reasonable steps to protect personal information it holds from misuse, interference and loss, and from unauthorised access, modification or disclosure.' The change from 'must' to 'should' and the removal of the stringency requirement weakens the obligation, potentially failing to meet the 'reasonable steps' required by APP 11.

perturbation.original_text: Following any request by NostraData , the Confidant must immediately provide to NostraData all Confidential Information and certify that no Confidential Information is retained by the Confidant . The obligation to return Confidential Information includ es the obligation to return any and all Confidential Information in physical, electronic or other tangible form.
perturbation.changed_text: Following a reasonable request by NostraData, the Confidant should provide Confidential Information to NostraData and provide some form of confirmation regarding the data's return. The obligation to return Confidential Information includes information in tangible form.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'any request' with 'a reasonable request' allows the Confidant to dispute the validity of a request. Changing 'must immediately provide' to 'should provide' weakens the obligation. Changing 'certify that no Confidential Information is retained' to 'provide some form of confirmation' makes the level of assurance unclear, potentially allowing for incomplete or inaccurate confirmation. Limiting to only 'tangible form' creates ambiguity if electronic records are considered.
perturbation.law_explanation: The original text ensured the complete removal and confirmation of no retained confidential information, providing strong data security. By weakening this requirement to 'some form of confirmation' and including 'reasonable request', it allows for potential breaches of data security if incomplete or inaccurate confirmation is provided, or requests are deemed unreasonable and ignored. APP 11 mandates entities must take steps to destroy or de-identify personal information when it is no longer needed.

This includes having a clearly expressed and up to date APP privacy policy . APP 2 Anonymity and pseudonymity Requires APP entities to give individuals the option of not identifying themselves, or of using a pseudonym. Limited exceptions apply. APP 3 Collection of solicited personal information Outlines when an APP entity can collect personal information that is solicited. It applies higher standards to the collection of sensitive information . APP 4 Dealing with unsolicited personal information Outlines how APP entities must deal with unsolicited personal information. An entity has obligations to destroy or de-identify personal information in certain circumstances. perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts APP 11, which mandates entities to take reasonable steps to protect personal information and destroy or de-identify it when no longer needed. The original text ensured complete removal and certification of no retained confidential information. By weakening this requirement to 'some form of confirmation' and including 'reasonable request', it allows for potential breaches of data security if incomplete or inaccurate confirmation is provided, or requests are deemed unreasonable and ignored. The changed text makes the obligation to return and confirm the destruction of confidential information less stringent, increasing the risk of unauthorized access, modification, or disclosure, which is in direct opposition to APP 11's requirements for data security.

perturbation.original_text: At the conclusion of the Datathon the Confi dant must permanently delete all data provided by NostraData . The data may be retained if NostraData makes specific requests for further demonstrations.
perturbation.changed_text: At the conclusion of the Datathon the Confidant should endeavor to delete data provided by NostraData. The data can be retained at the Confidant's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'must permanently delete' to 'should endeavor to delete' weakens the obligation to delete data. Replacing 'makes specific requests' with 'Confidant's discretion' allows the Confidant to retain data without NostraData's permission, undermining data security.
perturbation.law_explanation: APP 11 requires entities to take reasonable steps to destroy or de-identify personal information once it is no longer needed. Allowing the Confidant to retain data at their discretion contradicts this principle, potentially leading to unnecessary retention of personal information and increased privacy risks.
perturbation.location: 10 Deletion of data at the conclusion of the Datathon

perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates permanent deletion of data unless NostraData requests otherwise. The changed text weakens this obligation to an endeavor and allows data retention at the Confidant's discretion, irrespective of NostraData's consent. This contradicts APP 11, outlined in Scraped Law Snippet 2, which requires entities to take reasonable steps to protect personal information it holds from misuse, interference and loss, and from unauthorised access, modification or disclosure, and imposes obligations to destroy or de-identify personal information in certain circumstances.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidant must not reverse engineer or decompile any software included in any Confidential Information, or make any unauthorised copies of any Confidential Information. The Confidant must not attempt to re-identify any personal information that has been de -identified in the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidant should avoid reverse engineering or decompiling software included in Confidential Information and should also avoid re-identifying personal information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'must not' to 'should avoid' significantly weakens the prohibition on reverse engineering, decompiling, and re-identification. This introduces ambiguity as to whether these actions are strictly prohibited or merely discouraged.
perturbation.law_explanation: The original text strictly prohibited reverse engineering, decompilation, and re-identification. By weakening this to 'should avoid,' the Confidant is given leeway to potentially engage in these activities, which could lead to unauthorized access and disclosure of personal information. Re-identification of de-identified data directly contravenes the purpose of de-identification and increases the risk of privacy breaches. APP 11 requires active data protection, not merely avoidance of harmful actions.

perturbation.justification: The change from 'must not' to 'should avoid' weakens the prohibition against reverse engineering, decompiling, and re-identification. The Law Explanation suggests this contradicts APP 11, which mandates active data protection rather than merely avoiding harmful actions. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is missing, Scraped Law Snippet 2 explicitly states in APP 11 that an entity 'must take reasonable steps to protect personal information it holds from misuse, interference and loss, and from unauthorised access, modification or disclosure.' The original text aligned with this 'must take' obligation, whereas the changed text introduces ambiguity and potential non-compliance by only discouraging the prohibited activities, creating a legal contradiction as defined under the Ambiguities Legal Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidant may only use Confidential Information disclosed to it under this deed for the sole purpose of the Datathon and must use Confidential Information strictly in accordance with NostraData ’s directions and otherwise in accordance with this deed .
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidant may use Confidential Information disclosed under this deed primarily for the purpose of the Datathon and use Confidential Information generally in accordance with NostraData’s directions.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'only' to 'primarily' allows for use of Confidential Information outside of the Datathon. Replacing 'must use strictly in accordance' with 'use generally in accordance' loosens the requirement to follow NostraData's directions, potentially allowing the Confidant to deviate from the prescribed usage.
perturbation.law_explanation: The original text restricted the use of confidential information to the Datathon and required strict adherence to NostraData’s directions. The modified text weakens these restrictions, allowing the Confidant to use the information for other purposes and to deviate from NostraData’s instructions. APP 7 states that an organisation must only use or disclose personal information for the purpose for which it was collected.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Australian Privacy Principles quick reference | OAIC We use cookies on this site We use cookies to analyse traffic and to improve your browsing experience on our website. To find out more, read our privacy policy . Close Skip to main content If you have been impacted by the Qantas cyber incident, please view our statement . Australian Privacy Principles quick reference Download the poster (PDF, 614 KB) Principle Title Purpose APP 1 Open and transparent management of personal information Ensures that APP entities manage personal information in an open and transparent way. This includes having a clearly expressed and up to date APP privacy policy . APP 2 Anonymity and pseudonymity Requires APP entities to give individuals the option of not identifying themselves, or of using a pseudonym. Limited exceptions apply. APP 3 Collection of solicited personal information Outlines when an APP entity can collect personal information that is solicited. It applies higher standards to the collection of sensitive information . APP 4 Dealing with unsolicited personal information Outlines how APP entities must deal with unsolicited personal information. APP 5 Notification of the collection of personal information Outlines when and in what circumstances an APP entity that collects personal information must tell an individual about certain matters. APP 6 Use or disclosure of personal information Outlines the circumstances in which an APP entity may use or disclose personal information that it holds. APP 7 Direct marketing An organisation may only use or disclose personal information for direct marketing purposes if certain conditions are met. APP 8 Cross-border disclosure of personal information Outlines the steps an APP entity must take to protect personal information before it is disclosed overseas. APP 9 Adoption, use or disclosure of government related identifiers Outlines the limited circumstances when an organisation may adopt a government related identifier of an individual as its own identifier, or use or disclose a government related identifier of an individual. APP 10 Quality of personal information An APP entity must take reasonable steps to ensure the personal information it collects is accurate, up to date and complete. An entity must also take reasonable steps to ensure the personal information it uses or discloses is accurate, up to date, complete and relevant, having regard to the purpose of the use or disclosure. APP 11 Security of personal information An APP entity must take reasonable steps to protect personal information it holds from misuse, interference and loss, and from unauthorised access, modification or disclosure. An entity has obligations to destroy or de-identify personal information in certain circumstances. APP 12 Access to personal information Outlines an APP entity’s obligations when an individual requests to be given access to personal information held about them by the entity. This includes a requirement to provide access unless a specific exception applies. APP 13 Correction of personal information Outlines an APP entity’s obligations in relation to correcting the personal information it holds about individuals. perturbation.justification: The modification from 'only' to 'primarily' and 'must use strictly in accordance' to 'use generally in accordance' weakens the original restrictions on the use of confidential information. The original text limits the use to the Datathon and requires strict adherence to NostraData's directions. The changed text allows use outside the Datathon and deviation from NostraData's instructions, potentially violating APP 7, which, according to the scraped snippet, states that an organization may only use or disclose personal information for the purpose for which it was collected. The changed contract language introduces ambiguity and broadens the scope of permissible use, directly contradicting the 'only' use mandated by the original contract and implicitly by APP 7.
